_comment: L1 'handwritten ceiling row' (LFL-TERMINAL-RECIPES-THAT-SUCCEED-DESIGN.md section 6): hand-authored reference scripts for all 14 fixture goals in task-scenarios.json, using the expect/wait vocabulary added by lfl-terminal commit b348d03. Each body's own leading '#' comment line is its one-line rationale (parseScriptBody strips '#'-prefixed lines, so it never counts as a step). Every 'expect' step deliberately mirrors one of the goal's own harness-side success checks (url_contains -> expect url contains, text_visible -> expect text, field_value -> expect field ... equals), so a real run reports two independent verdicts (harness-side Python check vs in-product expect) for the same fact - see RESULTS-TASKS.md's human/fixture section for whether they ever disagreed. '{PORT}' is substituted at build time by build_human_authored.py with harness/runner.py's own PORT_A (default 8977), never hardcoded here, so this file stays correct if LFL_LAB_CORPUS_PORT_A is overridden. These bodies are validated against the REAL parseScriptBody() (via brainstorm/probe.py's validate_body(), same validator author_tasks.py's model-authored scripts go through) before being handed to task_runner.py - see that script's own header.

shop-open-blue-widget.body:
# rationale: browse to products, open Blue Widget by its exact link text, confirm arrival with expect (mirrors the harness's own url_contains/text_visible checks)
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
open "Blue Widget"
wait for heading "Blue Widget"
expect url contains "item-blue-widget"
expect text "BLUE-WIDGET-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-open-red-gadget.body:
# rationale: browse to products, open Red Gadget by its exact link text, confirm arrival with expect (mirrors the harness's own url_contains/text_visible checks)
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
open "Red Gadget"
wait for heading "Red Gadget"
expect url contains "item-red-gadget"
expect text "RED-GADGET-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-open-green-gizmo.body:
# rationale: browse to products, open Green Gizmo by its exact link text, confirm arrival with expect (mirrors the harness's own url_contains/text_visible checks)
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
open "Green Gizmo"
wait for heading "Green Gizmo"
expect url contains "item-green-gizmo"
expect text "GREEN-GIZMO-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-open-yellow-widget.body:
# rationale: browse to products, open Yellow Widget by its exact link text, confirm arrival with expect (mirrors the harness's own url_contains/text_visible checks)
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
open "Yellow Widget"
wait for heading "Yellow Widget"
expect url contains "item-yellow-widget"
expect text "YELLOW-WIDGET-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-search-open-blue.body:
# rationale: use the page's own search box before opening the named item from the (fixed) results list; wait-for guards each navigation, expect mirrors both harness checks
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
search "camping gear"
wait for heading "Search results"
expect url contains "search-results"
open "Blue Widget"
wait for heading "Blue Widget"
expect url contains "item-blue-widget"
expect text "BLUE-WIDGET-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-search-open-red.body:
# rationale: use the page's own search box before opening the named item from the (fixed) results list; wait-for guards each navigation, expect mirrors both harness checks
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
search "camping gear"
wait for heading "Search results"
expect url contains "search-results"
open "Red Gadget"
wait for heading "Red Gadget"
expect url contains "item-red-gadget"
expect text "RED-GADGET-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-search-open-green.body:
# rationale: use the page's own search box (a different query, same fixed results list) before opening the named item; wait-for guards each navigation, expect mirrors both harness checks
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
search "outdoor equipment"
wait for heading "Search results"
expect url contains "search-results"
open "Green Gizmo"
wait for heading "Green Gizmo"
expect url contains "item-green-gizmo"
expect text "GREEN-GIZMO-DETAIL-MARKER"

shop-search-third-pause.body:
# rationale: search, then pause before the index-addressed third-result click - parseScriptBody forbids scripting a bare position click, so pause hands control back exactly where the goal asks
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
search "camping gear"
wait for heading "Search results"
expect url contains "search-results"
expect text "SEARCH-RESULTS-MARKER"
pause "open the third search result yourself - its position cannot be scripted safely"

signup-contact-pause.body:
# rationale: fill name+email by label, expect confirms each field's live value before the designed pause hands back control for the submit click
go {PORT}/tasks/signup.html
wait for heading "Sign up for updates"
fill "Name" with "Jordan Rivera"
fill "Email" with "jordan@example.com"
expect field "Name" equals "Jordan Rivera"
expect field "Email" equals "jordan@example.com"
pause "review the sign-up form and submit it yourself"

signup-newsletter-pause.body:
# rationale: single-field fill by label, expect confirms the live value before the designed pause
go {PORT}/tasks/signup.html
wait for heading "Sign up for updates"
fill "Email" with "casey@example.com"
expect field "Email" equals "casey@example.com"
pause "review the sign-up form and submit it yourself"

signup-message-pause.body:
# rationale: fills the message textarea by label, expect confirms the trimmed live value before the designed pause
go {PORT}/tasks/signup.html
wait for heading "Sign up for updates"
fill "Message" with "Please add me to the trail newsletter"
expect field "Message" equals "Please add me to the trail newsletter"
pause "review the sign-up form and submit it yourself"

shop-scroll-item.body:
# rationale: opens the item then scrolls down as the goal literally asks; expect text targets the spec marker below the fold (isElementVisible here is CSS/DOM-based, not viewport-based, so the check would pass either way - the scroll step is kept anyway to honestly match what the goal text asks a human to do)
go {PORT}/tasks/products.html
wait for heading "Products"
open "Green Gizmo"
wait for heading "Green Gizmo"
expect url contains "item-green-gizmo"
scroll down
expect text "GREEN-GIZMO-SPEC-MARKER"

shop-open-item-back-to-products.body:
# rationale: full there-and-back path with an expect after every navigation - mirrors the harness's own end-state checks AND gives the steps_executed change-detector a non-navigating marker between navigations, which is exactly what README.md's documented lastResult-reset undercount needs to not misfire on a real 3+-step path
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Browse products"
wait for heading "Products"
expect text "PRODUCTS-LIST-MARKER"
open "Red Gadget"
wait for heading "Red Gadget"
expect url contains "item-red-gadget"
open "Back to all products"
wait for heading "Products"
expect url contains "products.html"
expect text "PRODUCTS-LIST-MARKER"

shop-open-signup.body:
# rationale: single hop by exact link text, expect mirrors both harness checks
go {PORT}/tasks/shop.html
open "Sign up for updates"
wait for heading "Sign up for updates"
expect url contains "signup.html"
expect text "Sign up for updates"
